rulings.Case Title: RUTH CASH et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 23, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Borg-Warner Corporation¿s motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. Plaintiffs¿ claims are for express indemnity and contribution rights that were assigned by Ford and GM from an April 2007 settlement. An action for contribution and indemnity is not governed by the statute of limitations applicable to the underlying tort action. (Great American West, Inc. v. Safeco Ins. (1991) 226 Cal.App.3d 1145, 1151). = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL LUNA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 23, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Westside Building Material Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any evidence creating a triable issue whether plaintiff worked with or around any products supplied by defendant. Mr. Rios¿ deposition testimony requires that his declaration be disregarded on this issue. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA BARNES et al VS. AC AND S, INC., et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 23, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Graybar Electric Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Cochrane¿s deposition testimony creates a triable issue whether Mr. Barnes was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The objections to Mr. Cochrane¿s deposition testimony are overruled. = (220/HEK)
